TypeSignature.Value: .class public auto ansi abstract beforefieldinit Attribute extends System.Object

Docs.summary: Represents the base class for custom attributes.

remarks.format:
## Remarks The <xref:System.Attribute> class associates predefined system information or user-defined custom information with a target element. A target element can be an assembly, class, constructor, delegate, enum, event, field, interface, method, portable executable file module, parameter, property, return value, struct, or another attribute.
Information provided by an attribute is also known as metadata. Metadata can be examined at run time by your application to control how your program processes data, or before run time by external tools to control how your application itself is processed or maintained. For example, .NET predefines and uses attribute types to control run-time behavior, and some programming languages use attribute types to represent language features not directly supported by the .NET common type system.
All attribute types derive directly or indirectly from the <xref:System.Attribute> class. Attributes can be applied to any target element; multiple attributes can be applied to the same target element; and attributes can be inherited by an element derived from a target element. Use the <xref:System.AttributeTargets> class to specify the target element to which the attribute is applied.
The <xref:System.Attribute> class provides convenient methods to retrieve and test custom attributes. For more information about using attributes, see [Applying Attributes](/dotnet/standard/attributes/applying-attributes) and [Attributes](/dotnet/standard/attributes/).
## Examples The following code example demonstrates the usage of <xref:System.Attribute>.
:::code language="csharp" source="~/snippets/csharp/System/Attribute/Overview/customattribute.cs" id="Snippet1"::: :::code language="fsharp" source="~/snippets/fsharp/VS_Snippets_CLR/AnimalAttributes/FS/customattribute.fs" id="Snippet1"::: :::code language="vb" source="~/snippets/visualbasic/System/Attribute/Overview/customattribute.vb" id="Snippet1":::

MemberSignature.Value: protected Attribute ();
.method familyhidebysig specialname rtspecialname instance void .ctor() cil managed
Protected Sub New ()
protected: Attribute();

Docs.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

remarks.format:
## Remarks This constructor is only called by classes that derive from <xref:System.Attribute>.
## Examples The following code example shows the definition of a custom parameter <xref:System.Attribute> class with its constructor.
:::code language="csharp" source="~/snippets/csharp/System/Attribute/.ctor/getcustattrparam.cs" id="Snippet2"::: :::code language="vb" source="~/snippets/visualbasic/System/Attribute/.ctor/getcustattrparam.vb" id="Snippet2":::

MemberSignature.Value: public override bool Equals (object? obj);
.method public hidebysig virtual instance bool Equals(object obj) cil managed
Public Overrides Function Equals (obj As Object) As Boolean
override this.Equals : obj -> bool
public: override bool Equals(System::Object ^ obj);
public override bool Equals (object obj);

Docs.param: An to compare with this instance or .
Docs.summary: Returns a value that indicates whether this instance is equal to a specified object.
Docs.returns: if and this instance are of the same type and have identical field values; otherwise, .

remarks.format:
## Remarks
The <xref:System.Attribute.Equals*> method uses reflection to retrieve field information for the current instance and the `obj` argument. It then compares field values.
When implementing your own class derived from <xref:System.Attribute>, you can override the <xref:System.Attribute.Equals*> method. Because it uses reflection, we recommend that you do so. However, your override should perform a standard test for reference equality (the two arguments represent the same object instance) or value equality (the two arguments are of the same type and have identical field values). If you want to perform a custom comparison to determine whether two attributes objects are equal, you can override the <xref:System.Attribute.Match*> method.
## Examples The following example defines two custom parameter <xref:System.Attribute> classes, then creates several objects of each class and uses the <xref:System.Attribute.Equals*> method to compare them.
:::code language="csharp" source="~/snippets/csharp/System/Attribute/Equals/equals.cs" interactive="try-dotnet"::: :::code language="fsharp" source="~/snippets/fsharp/VS_Snippets_CLR_System/system.Attribute.Equals/FS/equals.fs"::: :::code language="vb" source="~/snippets/visualbasic/System/Attribute/Equals/equals.vb":::

Docs.summary: Retrieves a custom attribute of a specified type applied to an assembly, module, type member, or method parameter.

MemberSignature.Value: public static Attribute? GetCustomAttribute (System.Reflection.Assembly element, Type attributeType);
.method public static hidebysig class System.Attribute GetCustomAttribute(class System.Reflection.Assembly element, class System.Type attributeType) cil managed
Public Shared Function GetCustomAttribute (element As Assembly, attributeType As Type) As Attribute
static member GetCustomAttribute : System.Reflection.Assembly * Type -> Attribute
public: static Attribute ^ GetCustomAttribute(System::Reflection::Assembly ^ element, Type ^ attributeType);
public static Attribute GetCustomAttribute (System.Reflection.Assembly element, Type attributeType);

Docs.param: An object derived from the class that describes a reusable collection of modules.
The type, or a base type, of the custom attribute to search for.
Docs.summary: Retrieves a custom attribute applied to a specified assembly. Parameters specify the assembly and the type of the custom attribute to search for.
Docs.returns: A reference to the single custom attribute of type that is applied to , or if there is no such attribute.

remarks.format:
## Remarks Use the <xref:System.Attribute.GetCustomAttributes*> method if you expect more than one value to be returned, or <xref:System.Reflection.AmbiguousMatchException> will be thrown.
## Examples The following code example illustrates the use of the <xref:System.Attribute.GetCustomAttribute*> method taking an <xref:System.Reflection.Assembly> as a parameter.
:::code language="csharp" source="~/snippets/csharp/System/Attribute/GetCustomAttribute/id1.cs" id="Snippet1"::: :::code language="fsharp" source="~/snippets/fsharp/VS_Snippets_CLR/IsDefined/FS/id1.fs" id="Snippet1"::: :::code language="vb" source="~/snippets/visualbasic/System/Attribute/GetCustomAttribute/id1.vb" id="Snippet1":::

Docs.exception: More than one of the requested attributes was found.

MemberSignature.Value: public static Attribute? GetCustomAttribute (System.Reflection.MemberInfo element, Type attributeType);
.method public static hidebysig class System.Attribute GetCustomAttribute(class System.Reflection.MemberInfo element, class System.Type attributeType) cil managed
Public Shared Function GetCustomAttribute (element As MemberInfo, attributeType As Type) As Attribute
static member GetCustomAttribute : System.Reflection.MemberInfo * Type -> Attribute
public: static Attribute ^ GetCustomAttribute(System::Reflection::MemberInfo ^ element, Type ^ attributeType);
public static Attribute GetCustomAttribute (System.Reflection.MemberInfo element, Type attributeType);

Docs.param: An object derived from the class that describes a constructor, event, field, method, or property member of a class.
The type, or a base type, of the custom attribute to search for.
Docs.summary: Retrieves a custom attribute applied to a member of a type. Parameters specify the member, and the type of the custom attribute to search for.
Docs.returns: A reference to the single custom attribute of type that is applied to , or if there is no such attribute.

remarks.format:
## Remarks A match is determined in the same way described in the Return Value section of <xref:System.Type.IsAssignableFrom*?displayProperty=nameWithType>.
## Examples The following code example illustrates the use of the <xref:System.Attribute.GetCustomAttribute*> method taking a <xref:System.Reflection.MemberInfo> as a parameter.
:::code language="csharp" source="~/snippets/csharp/System/Attribute/GetCustomAttribute/id4.cs" id="Snippet4"::: :::code language="fsharp" source="~/snippets/fsharp/VS_Snippets_CLR/IsDefined/FS/id4.fs" id="Snippet4"::: :::code language="vb" source="~/snippets/visualbasic/System/Attribute/GetCustomAttribute/id4.vb" id="Snippet4":::

Docs.exception: is not a constructor, method, property, event, type, or field.
More than one of the requested attributes was found.
A custom attribute type cannot be loaded.

MemberSignature.Value: public static Attribute? GetCustomAttribute (System.Reflection.ParameterInfo element, Type attributeType);
.method public static hidebysig class System.Attribute GetCustomAttribute(class System.Reflection.ParameterInfo element, class System.Type attributeType) cil managed
Public Shared Function GetCustomAttribute (element As ParameterInfo, attributeType As Type) As Attribute
static member GetCustomAttribute : System.Reflection.ParameterInfo * Type -> Attribute
public: static Attribute ^ GetCustomAttribute(System::Reflection::ParameterInfo ^ element, Type ^ attributeType);
public static Attribute GetCustomAttribute (System.Reflection.ParameterInfo element, Type attributeType);

Docs.param: An object derived from the class that describes a parameter of a member of a class.
The type, or a base type, of the custom attribute to search for.
Docs.summary: Retrieves a custom attribute applied to a method parameter. Parameters specify the method parameter, and the type of the custom attribute to search for.
Docs.returns: A reference to the single custom attribute of type that is applied to , or if there is no such attribute.

remarks.format:
## Remarks If `element` represents a parameter in a method of a derived type, the return value includes the inheritable custom attributes applied to the same parameter in the overridden base methods.
## Examples The following code example defines a custom parameter <xref:System.Attribute> class and applies the custom attribute to a method in a derived class and the base of the derived class. The example shows the use of the <xref:System.Attribute.GetCustomAttribute*> method to return the attributes.
:::code language="csharp" source="~/snippets/csharp/System/Attribute/.ctor/getcustattrparam.cs" id="Snippet1"::: :::code language="fsharp" source="~/snippets/fsharp/VS_Snippets_CLR_System/system.Attribute.GetCustomAttribute/FS/getcustattrparam.fs" id="Snippet1"::: :::code language="vb" source="~/snippets/visualbasic/System/Attribute/.ctor/getcustattrparam.vb" id="Snippet1":::

Docs.exception: More than one of the requested attributes was found.
A custom attribute type cannot be loaded.

MemberSignature.Value: public static Attribute? GetCustomAttribute (System.Reflection.Assembly element, Type attributeType, bool inherit);
.method public static hidebysig class System.Attribute GetCustomAttribute(class System.Reflection.Assembly element, class System.Type attributeType, bool inherit) cil managed
Public Shared Function GetCustomAttribute (element As Assembly, attributeType As Type, inherit As Boolean) As Attribute
static member GetCustomAttribute : System.Reflection.Assembly * Type * bool -> Attribute
public: static Attribute ^ GetCustomAttribute(System::Reflection::Assembly ^ element, Type ^ attributeType, bool inherit);
public static Attribute GetCustomAttribute (System.Reflection.Assembly element, Type attributeType, bool inherit);

Docs.param: An object derived from the class that describes a reusable collection of modules.
The type, or a base type, of the custom attribute to search for.
This parameter is ignored, and does not affect the operation of this method.
Docs.summary: Retrieves a custom attribute applied to an assembly. Parameters specify the assembly, the type of the custom attribute to search for, and an ignored search option.
Docs.returns: A reference to the single custom attribute of type that is applied to , or if there is no such attribute.

remarks.format:
## Examples The following code example illustrates the use of the <xref:System.Attribute.GetCustomAttribute*> method taking an <xref:System.Reflection.Assembly> as a parameter.

MemberSignature.Value: public static Attribute? GetCustomAttribute (System.Reflection.MemberInfo element, Type attributeType, bool inherit);

Public Shared Function GetCustomAttribute (element As MemberInfo, attributeType As Type, inherit As Boolean) As Attribute

public: static Attribute ^ GetCustomAttribute(System::Reflection::MemberInfo ^ element, Type ^ attributeType, bool inherit);
public static Attribute GetCustomAttribute (System.Reflection.MemberInfo element, Type attributeType, bool inherit);

Docs.param: An object derived from the class that describes a constructor, event, field, method, or property member of a class.
The type, or a base type, of the custom attribute to search for.
If , specifies to also search the ancestors of for custom attributes.
Docs.summary: Retrieves a custom attribute applied to a member of a type. Parameters specify the member, the type of the custom attribute to search for, and whether to search ancestors of the member.
Docs.returns: A reference to the single custom attribute of type that is applied to , or if there is no such attribute.

If , specifies to also search the ancestors of for custom attributes.

Docs.returns: A reference to the single custom attribute of type that is applied to , or if there is no such attribute.

remarks.format:
## Remarks If `element` represents a parameter in a method of a derived type, the return value includes the inheritable custom attributes applied to the same parameter in the overridden base methods.
## Examples The following code example defines a custom parameter <xref:System.Attribute> class and applies the custom attribute to a method in a derived class and the base of the derived class. The example shows the use of the <xref:System.Attribute.GetCustomAttribute*> method to return the attributes.
:::code language="csharp" source="~/snippets/csharp/System/Attribute/.ctor/getcustattrprminh.cs" id="Snippet3"::: :::code language="fsharp" source="~/snippets/fsharp/VS_Snippets_CLR_System/system.Attribute.GetCustomAttribute/FS/getcustattrprminh.fs" id="Snippet3"::: :::code language="vb" source="~/snippets/visualbasic/System/Attribute/.ctor/getcustattrprminh.vb" id="Snippet3":::

Docs.summary: Retrieves an array of the custom attributes applied to an assembly, module, type member, or method parameter.

MemberSignature.Value: public static Attribute[] GetCustomAttributes (System.Reflection.Assembly element);
.method public static hidebysig class System.Attribute[] GetCustomAttributes(class System.Reflection.Assembly element) cil managed
Public Shared Function GetCustomAttributes (element As Assembly) As Attribute()
static member GetCustomAttributes : System.Reflection.Assembly -> Attribute[]
public: static cli::array <Attribute ^> ^ GetCustomAttributes(System::Reflection::Assembly ^ element);

Docs.param: An object derived from the class that describes a reusable collection of modules.
Docs.summary: Retrieves an array of the custom attributes applied to an assembly. A parameter specifies the assembly.
Docs.returns: An array that contains the custom attributes applied to , or an empty array if no such custom attributes exist.

remarks.format:
## Examples The following example retrieves the custom attributes found in the current assembly.
:::code language="csharp" source="~/snippets/csharp/System/Attribute/GetCustomAttributes/ca1.cs" id="Snippet1"::: :::code language="fsharp" source="~/snippets/fsharp/VS_Snippets_CLR/GetCustomAttributes/FS/ca1.fs" id="Snippet1"::: :::code language="vb" source="~/snippets/visualbasic/System/Attribute/GetCustomAttributes/ca1.vb" id="Snippet1":::

.method public static hidebysig class System.Attribute[] GetCustomAttributes(class System.Reflection.MemberInfo element) cil managed
Public Shared Function GetCustomAttributes (element As MemberInfo) As Attribute()
static member GetCustomAttributes : System.Reflection.MemberInfo -> Attribute[]
public: static cli::array <Attribute ^> ^ GetCustomAttributes(System::Reflection::MemberInfo ^ element);

Docs.param: An object derived from the class that describes a constructor, event, field, method, or property member of a class.
Docs.summary: Retrieves an array of the custom attributes applied to a member of a type. A parameter specifies the member.
Docs.returns: An array that contains the custom attributes applied to , or an empty array if no such custom attributes exist.

remarks.format:
## Remarks The return value contains the custom attributes for ancestors of `element`.
## Examples The following code example demonstrates the use of <xref:System.Attribute.GetCustomAttribute*>, taking a <xref:System.Reflection.MemberInfo> as a parameter.

This parameter is ignored, and does not affect the operation of this method.
Docs.summary: Retrieves an array of the custom attributes applied to a module. Parameters specify the module, and an ignored search option.
Docs.returns: An array that contains the custom attributes applied to , or an empty array if no such custom attributes exist.

remarks.format:
## Remarks The return value contains the custom attributes for ancestors of `element` if `inherit` is `true`.
## Examples The following code example demonstrates the use of <xref:System.Attribute.GetCustomAttributes*>, taking a <xref:System.Reflection.Module> as a parameter.
:::code language="csharp" source="~/snippets/csharp/System/Attribute/GetCustomAttributes/ca2.cs" id="Snippet2"::: :::code language="fsharp" source="~/snippets/fsharp/VS_Snippets_CLR/GetCustomAttributes/FS/ca2.fs" id="Snippet2"::: :::code language="vb" source="~/snippets/visualbasic/System/Attribute/GetCustomAttributes/ca2.vb" id="Snippet2":::

Public Shared Function GetCustomAttributes (element As Module, attributeType As Type) As Attribute()
static member GetCustomAttributes : System.Reflection.Module * Type -> Attribute[]
public: static cli::array <Attribute ^> ^ GetCustomAttributes(System::Reflection::Module ^ element, Type ^ attributeType);

Docs.param: An object derived from the class that describes a portable executable file.
The type, or a base type, of the custom attribute to search for.
Docs.summary: Retrieves an array of the custom attributes applied to a module. Parameters specify the module, and the type of the custom attribute to search for.
Docs.returns: An array that contains the custom attributes of type applied to , or an empty array if no such custom attributes exist.

remarks.format:

MemberSignature.Value: public static Attribute[] GetCustomAttributes (System.Reflection.ParameterInfo element, bool inherit);
.method public static hidebysig class System.Attribute[] GetCustomAttributes(class System.Reflection.ParameterInfo element, bool inherit) cil managed
Public Shared Function GetCustomAttributes (element As ParameterInfo, inherit As Boolean) As Attribute()
static member GetCustomAttributes : System.Reflection.ParameterInfo * bool -> Attribute[]
public: static cli::array <Attribute ^> ^ GetCustomAttributes(System::Reflection::ParameterInfo ^ element, bool inherit);

Docs.param: An object derived from the class that describes a parameter of a member of a class.
If , specifies to also search the ancestors of for custom attributes.
Docs.summary: Retrieves an array of the custom attributes applied to a method parameter. Parameters specify the method parameter, and whether to search ancestors of the method parameter.
Docs.returns: An array that contains the custom attributes applied to , or an empty array if no such custom attributes exist.

remarks.format:
## Remarks If `element` represents a parameter in a method of a derived type, the return value includes the inheritable custom attributes applied to the same parameter in the overridden base methods.
## Examples The following code example demonstrates the use of <xref:System.Attribute.GetCustomAttributes*>, taking a <xref:System.Reflection.ParameterInfo> as a parameter.
:::code language="csharp" source="~/snippets/csharp/System/Attribute/GetCustomAttributes/ca5.cs" id="Snippet5"::: :::code language="fsharp" source="~/snippets/fsharp/VS_Snippets_CLR/GetCustomAttributes/FS/ca5.fs" id="Snippet5"::: :::code language="vb" source="~/snippets/visualbasic/System/Attribute/GetCustomAttributes/ca5.vb" id="Snippet5":::

Docs.exception: A custom attribute type cannot be loaded.

MemberSignature.Value: public override int GetHashCode ();
.method public hidebysig virtual instance int32 GetHashCode() cil managed
Public Overrides Function GetHashCode () As Integer
override this.GetHashCode : unit -> int
public: override int GetHashCode();

Docs.summary: Returns the hash code for this instance.
Docs.returns: A 32-bit signed integer hash code.

remarks.format:
## Examples The following code example illustrates the use of <xref:System.Attribute.GetHashCode*> in the context of <xref:System.Attribute>.
:::code language="csharp" source="~/snippets/csharp/System/Attribute/GetHashCode/hashcode.cs" id="Snippet1"::: :::code language="fsharp" source="~/snippets/fsharp/VS_Snippets_CLR/GetHashCode/FS/hashcode.fs" id="Snippet1"::: :::code language="vb" source="~/snippets/visualbasic/System/Attribute/GetHashCode/hashcode.vb" id="Snippet1":::

MemberSignature.Value: public virtual bool IsDefaultAttribute ();
.method public hidebysig newslot virtual instance bool IsDefaultAttribute() cil managed
Public Overridable Function IsDefaultAttribute () As Boolean
abstract member IsDefaultAttribute : unit -> bool override this.IsDefaultAttribute : unit -> bool
public: virtual bool IsDefaultAttribute();

Docs.summary: When overridden in a derived class, indicates whether the value of this instance is the default value for the derived class.
Docs.returns: if this instance is the default attribute for the class; otherwise, .

remarks.format:
## Remarks The default implementation of this class returns `false`, and must be implemented in the derived class to be useful to that class.
The implementation of this method in a derived class compares the value of this instance to a standard default value, then returns a Boolean value that indicates whether the value of this instance is equal to the standard value. The standard value is typically coded as a constant in the implementation, or stored programmatically in a field used by the implementation.
## Examples The following code example illustrates the use of <xref:System.Attribute.IsDefaultAttribute*>.
:::code language="csharp" source="~/snippets/csharp/System/Attribute/IsDefaultAttribute/defattr.cs" id="Snippet1"::: :::code language="fsharp" source="~/snippets/fsharp/VS_Snippets_CLR/IsDefaultAttribute/FS/defattr.fs" id="Snippet1"::: :::code language="vb" source="~/snippets/visualbasic/System/Attribute/IsDefaultAttribute/defattr.vb" id="Snippet1":::

Docs.summary: Determines whether any custom attributes of a specified type are applied to an assembly, module, type member, or method parameter.

Docs.returns: if a custom attribute of type is applied to ; otherwise, .

remarks.format:
## Examples The following code example illustrates the use of <xref:System.Attribute.IsDefined*>, taking a <xref:System.Reflection.ParameterInfo> as a parameter.
:::code language="csharp" source="~/snippets/csharp/System/Attribute/GetCustomAttribute/id5.cs" id="Snippet5"::: :::code language="fsharp" source="~/snippets/fsharp/VS_Snippets_CLR/IsDefined/FS/id5.fs" id="Snippet5"::: :::code language="vb" source="~/snippets/visualbasic/System/Attribute/GetCustomAttribute/id5.vb" id="Snippet5":::

Docs.exception: is not a method, constructor, or type.

MemberSignature.Value: public virtual bool Match (object? obj);
.method public hidebysig newslot virtual instance bool Match(object obj) cil managed
Public Overridable Function Match (obj As Object) As Boolean
abstract member Match : obj -> bool override this.Match : obj -> bool
public: virtual bool Match(System::Object ^ obj);
public virtual bool Match (object obj);

Docs.param: An to compare with this instance of .
Docs.summary: When overridden in a derived class, returns a value that indicates whether this instance equals a specified object.
Docs.returns: if this instance equals ; otherwise, .

remarks.format:
## Remarks This method determines if one <xref:System.Attribute> equals another. Its default implementation is the same as <xref:System.Attribute.Equals*>, which determines whether two attributes are of the same type and have the same field values.
In general, the <xref:System.Attribute.Equals*> method is intended to perform a standard test for reference or value equality. You can override the <xref:System.Attribute.Match*> method when you want to perform a custom comparison for equality that is based on some criteria other than that two attribute instances are of the same type and have identical values. For example, you can override the <xref:System.Attribute.Match*> method to compare attributes in the following scenarios:
- An attribute includes a bit field, and you consider two attribute instances equal if a particular bit is set. For example, a `NumericDisplay` attribute might include a bit field that indicates what numeric formats (such as binary, octal, decimal, and hexadecimal) a client supports. The overridden <xref:System.Attribute.Match*> method might consider two instances equal if they support the same numeric format.
- An attribute includes multiple fields that contain the same kind of information, or it contains an array in which values can be in any order. For example, an `Author` attribute might include multiple fields for author names. The overridden <xref:System.Attribute.Match*> method might consider two instances equal if they have the same authors regardless of whether each field is equal to the corresponding field.
## Example The following example illustrates the use of <xref:System.Attribute.Match*> to create a custom comparison method for attribute values. If defines an `AuthorsAttribute` that internally contains a `List<String>` that stores authors' names. Because the names can occur in any order in the list, it overrides the <xref:System.Attribute.Match*> method to compare author names regardless of their position in the list. Note the <xref:System.Attribute.Equals*> method, which performs a test for value equality, returns different results from the <xref:System.Attribute.Match*> method.
:::code language="csharp" source="~/snippets/csharp/System/Attribute/Match/match.cs" interactive="try-dotnet"::: :::code language="fsharp" source="~/snippets/fsharp/VS_Snippets_CLR/Match/FS/match.fs"::: [!code-vb[Match#1](~/snippets/visualbasic/System/Attribute/Match/match.vb)]

MemberSignature.Value: public virtual object TypeId { get; }
.property instance object TypeId
Public Overridable ReadOnly Property TypeId As Object
member this.TypeId : obj
public: virtual property System::Object ^ TypeId { System::Object ^ get(); };

Docs.summary: When implemented in a derived class, gets a unique identifier for this .
Docs.value: An that is a unique identifier for the attribute.

remarks.format:
## Remarks As implemented, this identifier is merely the <xref:System.Type> of the attribute. However, it is intended that the unique identifier be used to identify two attributes of the same type.
## Examples The following code example implements the <xref:System.Attribute.TypeId> property in a custom parameter <xref:System.Attribute> class and shows its use.
:::code language="csharp" source="~/snippets/csharp/System/Attribute/TypeId/typeid.cs" id="Snippet1"::: :::code language="fsharp" source="~/snippets/fsharp/VS_Snippets_CLR_System/system.Attribute.TypeId/FS/typeid.fs" id="Snippet1"::: :::code language="vb" source="~/snippets/visualbasic/System/Attribute/TypeId/typeid.vb" id="Snippet1":::
